result.search_summary: Initial pass surfaced pain around shipment update coordination.

raw_signals.verbatim_quote: We still push shipment exceptions around in spreadsheets every day.
Own inbound exception management across carrier, warehouse, and customer teams.
raw_signals.inferred_pain: Companies hire people to coordinate exceptions manually.

raw_signals.verbatim_quote: Customers escalate when update windows slip and nobody knows the source of delay.
raw_signals.inferred_pain: Exception visibility gaps create customer escalations.

result.search_summary: Targeted pass found explicit budget signals and incumbent spend.

raw_signals.verbatim_quote: Experience with paid TMS and workflow automation tooling required.
raw_signals.inferred_pain: Teams already budget for logistics workflow tooling.

raw_signals.verbatim_quote: Mid-market TMS buyers typically pay for exception dashboards and carrier integrations.
raw_signals.inferred_pain: Exception management already has budget and pricing anchors.

problem_units.job_to_be_done: Coordinate shipment exception updates across carrier, warehouse, and customer teams
problem_units.trigger_event: A shipment misses its planned handoff or ETA
problem_units.urgency: Customers escalate quickly when updates are late
problem_units.cost_of_failure: Lost time, angry customers, and avoidable credits

result.clustering_summary: The evidence converges on one clear coordination problem.

entries.lesson_for_us: A narrow wedge focused on exception coordination can differentiate.

entries.what_they_do: Tried to automate carrier update emails.
entries.failure_reason: Too horizontal and not tied to a workflow owner
entries.lesson_for_us: Own the workflow and measurable SLA improvement, not generic notifications.

result.scoring_summary: The candidate now clears Gate A.

risk_flags: Direct budget evidence is still thin.
missing_evidence: Find pricing or job-posting evidence that this workflow already has paid tooling.
result.landscape_detail: Incumbents exist, but most are broad platforms with heavy implementation.
result.recommendation_rationale: The wedge should not move forward until spend evidence is clearer.

risk_flags: Switching friction will need onboarding support.
result.landscape_detail: The market is active, but the problem is still underserved in the exception workflow layer.
result.recommendation_rationale: The candidate is now strong enough to design a wedge.

wedges.wedge_promise: We automate shipment exception updates so ops teams stop losing hours to spreadsheet follow-up.
wedges.key_capability: Monitors shipment exceptions, assigns owners, and generates update workflows.
wedges.target_outcome: Cut exception update handling time by 50 percent.
wedges.differentiation: Focused on the exception workflow rather than broad TMS replacement.
wedges.mvp_scope: Carrier and warehouse update orchestration for exception events only.
wedges.first_10_onboarding: Import active shipments and connect one carrier feed.
wedges.switching_ease: Runs beside existing TMS without replacing it.
wedges.data_advantage: Builds a dataset on exception timing and response workflows.

wedges.wedge_promise: We summarize shipment delays into weekly reports for ops leaders.
wedges.key_capability: Aggregates exception activity into management reports.
wedges.first_10_onboarding: Upload CSV exports once per week.

result.design_rationale: The best wedge focuses on urgent exception handling, not passive reporting.

wedges.wedge_promise: We automate shipment exception triage so ops teams deliver faster customer updates without replacing their TMS.
wedges.key_capability: Pulls exception events from existing systems, assigns next actions, and drafts customer-facing updates.
wedges.target_outcome: Reduce update lag and customer escalations within 30 days.
wedges.differentiation: Adds a focused workflow layer on top of incumbent systems instead of competing as a full platform.
wedges.mvp_scope: Exception intake, triage, and outbound update workflow for one warehouse operation.
wedges.first_10_onboarding: Connect one existing TMS feed and map one escalation workflow.
wedges.switching_ease: Sidecar rollout with one team before expansion.
wedges.data_advantage: Captures structured exception-response timing and outcomes.

result.design_rationale: The revision tightens the promise around customer updates and sidecar rollout.

key_issues: The sidecar positioning needs to be clearer.
Weekly reporting is too far from the urgent pain.

revision_suggestions: State explicitly that the wedge layers on top of incumbent systems.
Tie the outcome to customer update speed and escalation reduction.
result.overall_summary: One wedge is close, but it needs sharper positioning.

key_issues: Need one channel with clear buyer access.

result.overall_summary: The revised wedge is viable and ready for buyer/channel validation.

result.procurement_notes: Mid-market buyers can approve sidecar tooling quickly when the rollout is team-scoped.

channels.how_to_reach: Cold email Heads of Ops via Apollo-style logistics lists
channels.lead_source: Apollo filtered by warehouse and logistics ops titles
channels.message_angle: Reduce update lag without replacing the TMS
channels.first_20_plan: Pull 20 mid-market ops leaders and pitch the sidecar workflow angle.

result.verdict_rationale: Email alone is too thin and expensive.

result.procurement_notes: The sidecar rollout and low initial integration burden keep procurement light.

channels.how_to_reach: Cold email Heads of Ops via filtered logistics lists
channels.message_angle: Reduce shipment update lag without a rip-and-replace project
channels.first_20_plan: Start with 20 Heads of Ops across 3PLs and warehouse-heavy distributors.

channels.how_to_reach: Founder-led LinkedIn outreach to VP Operations and logistics leaders
channels.lead_source: LinkedIn Sales Navigator title and company filters
channels.message_angle: Show how the sidecar workflow cuts customer escalations
channels.first_20_plan: Follow email with ten warm-looking LinkedIn prospects and use the escalation metric angle.

result.verdict_rationale: Two channels and a clear buyer map make the wedge reachable.
